rationale: Both papers examine nonstandard language in marketing text and consumer responses (citing: brand slang effects on attitudes/engagement; cited: swear-word effects on review helpfulness/attitudes), making the cited work a plausible theory/comparison anchor rather than a purely incidental mention. The citing note explicitly positions its contribution against prior language-use literature in marketing communication, and this 2022 JMR profanity paper is directly in that space. With an exact DOI match (10.1177/00222437221078606), this pair should be retained for full relation assessment.
